name: machine-pwm
description: Pulse Width Modulation output via ESP32 LEDC peripheral. Control LED brightness, servo motors, and analog-like outputs.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific PWM via ESP32 hardware LEDC controller.

classes.name: PWM

constructor_params.name: pin
constructor_params.description: Pin object. Required. Must have Pin object, not raw GPIO number.

methods.name: PWM.deinit
methods.description: Turn off PWM on this pin. Releases LEDC channel.

methods.name: PWM.freq
methods.description: Get or set PWM frequency in Hz.

methods.name: PWM.duty_u16
methods.description: Get or set duty cycle as 16-bit value (0-65535).

methods.name: PWM.duty_ns
methods.description: Get or set duty cycle in nanoseconds. Requires freq to be set first.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin, PWM

# LED brightness control
led = PWM(Pin(2), freq=1000)
led.duty(512)    # ~50% brightness
led.duty(0)      # off
led.duty(1023)   # full brightness

# Servo control (50Hz)
servo = PWM(Pin(15), freq=50)
servo.duty_ns(1500000)  # 1.5ms pulse = center position
servo.duty_ns(1000000)  # 1.0ms = 0 degrees
servo.duty_ns(2000000)  # 2.0ms = 180 degrees

esp32_notes: Higher frequencies reduce duty resolution: freq * 2^bit_depth <= 80MHz.
duty_ns() requires freq to be set first — otherwise Unknown.
Each PWM object consumes one LEDC channel; deinit() to free it.
